items.title: Touring artists wind back shows as fuel crisis bites

items.summary: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

items.title: Falling consumption, rising costs and tariffs take bounce out of yellow tail winemaker

items.title: Australia women lose Hong Kong rugby sevens final to New Zealand, men finish sixth
items.summary: The Black Ferns Sevens beat Australia's women's rugby sevens team to win the Hong Kong title, marking the ninth time in the past 10 finals that the Kiwis have come out on top.

items.title: Iran daily briefing: Why JD Vance might have no-one to talk to, plus Israel's new 'yellow line'
items.summary: Welcome to Monday and a return to the rhetorical threat spiral we've been watching for weeks. Here's what you should know this morning.

items.title: Lifeblood's blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

items.title: Tasmanian Labor says it's 'back' after multiple election defeats, but there's still a long road ahead

items.title: Sydney kids turn struggle with school into ambitious art show
items.summary: At an art gallery in Sydney's inner west, a group of marginalised children are staging their first exhibition — and for many of them, it is the first time they have felt seen.

items.title: Louisiana domestic violence shooting leaves eight children dead in Shreveport
items.summary: The victims, who were shot at two homes in the US state of Louisiana, ranged in age from one to about 14 years old.

items.title: Is free public transport enough to convert motorists? Research says there are more important factors

items.summary: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

items.title: ADHD rates in adults are skyrocketing — but by how much depends where you live

items.title: With Iran and the Strait of Hormuz still in flux, China's bet on renewable energy is paying off

items.summary: Expert advice on how to handle council trees causing problems on private property.

items.title: Peter and Rosie use power of song and an appearance on The Piano to save endangered language

items.title: Australia's 'lucky country' mantle has slipped — and we're not happy about it

items.title: Carlton aware Elijah Hollands was 'struggling in the game' as AFL demands answers after mental health episode

items.title: Demons cling on for epic win over Lions as Freo makes light work of Eagles

items.summary: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith issues his first statement since his release on bail from a Sydney jail.

items.title: Love story: I fell in love with my daughter's father two years after she was born

items.title: NSW Police investigate two alleged murders in Lake Macquarie
items.summary: A man charged with an alleged domestic violence murder of a woman, believed to be his mother, is refused bail. Meanwhile, police are calling for public help after a man was stabbed by masked intruders at Argenton.

items.title: Remote NT communities appeal to governments for fuel security assurances, price relief as costs soar
items.summary: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

items.title: Iran's reclosure of the Strait of Hormuz 'disappointing', Marles says
items.summary: The deputy prime minister says "every diplomatic angle" should be used to secure a permanent ceasefire in the Middle East.

items.title: Couple makes handmade skis out of Australian-grown timber in NSW Snowy Mountains
items.summary: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

items.summary: Young Socceroo Mohamed Toure continues to shine in the lead-up to the World Cup, scoring another hat-trick for English Championship side Norwich.

items.title: Thousands of dollars worth of belongings stolen from cars shipped across Australia, but no-one held responsible
items.summary: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

items.title: Man faces court charged with culpable driving causing death after Melbourne pedestrian death
items.summary: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

items.title: Fantasy becomes reality for next-gen speculative fiction authors riding self-publishing boom
items.summary: Is your dream to become the next JK Rowling or Stephanie Meyer? A new wave of writers is turning to self-publishing to turn fantasy into reality.

items.title: Head coach of the Tasmania Devils Women's VFL side's tarot reading pastime
items.summary: Georgia Walker describes herself as a psychic medium, which she combines with her skills as a counsellor and teacher, as head coach of the Tasmania Devils' women's VFL team.

items.title: Fuel costs no obstacle as West Australian aurora chasers hit the road
items.summary: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

items.title: Some Royal Darwin Hospital staff use ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim
items.summary: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

items.title: World's largest live export ship sails out of Darwin with 17,000 cattle bound for Indonesia
items.summary: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

items.title: Kimberley diphtheria rates more than triple in a month after 50-year eradication
items.summary: An outbreak of the long-dormant, highly contagious bacterial infection diphtheria in Western Australia's north is raising alarm among government officials and experts.

items.title: Liberal candidate under investigation for roadworks ahead of Nepean by-election
items.summary: The Liberal Party's candidate for the crucial upcoming Nepean by-election is being investigated by Victoria's transport department for unauthorised roadworks in a campaign stunt with Opposition Leader Jess Wilson.

items.title: Record earthquake near Cadia gold mine sparks calls for more monitors
items.summary: The magnitude 4.5-earthquake near Orange was logged as the region's largest on record, but only thanks to public monitoring data far away from the epicentre.

items.title: Women's shelters turn curated second-hand fashion into fundraising for frontlines services
items.summary: Women's shelters are having to get creative to acquire necessary funding such as turning fashion into sustaining frontline support.

items.title: Adelaide Crows to give Jordan Dawson time before AFL return, Matthew Nicks says
items.summary: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

items.title: Pale octopus species mysteriously disappears from SA's Spencer Gulf
items.summary: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

items.title: Visits to NSW national parks hit record high with more young people heading outdoors, data shows
items.summary: The latest NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service Park Visitation Survey shows there were 65.5 million visits to the state's parks over the 2024-25 financial year.

items.title: 'Old bushie' Steve Hawe has a dream to make his board game an Aussie classic
items.summary: Steve Hawe says he never set out to make a board game — but when inspiration struck, he couldn't ignore it.

items.title: Analysts raise doubts about America's ability to clear mines from Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The prospect of mines in the Strait of Hormuz has highlighted a gap in US naval capabilities and reinforced its need for NATO help.

items.title: How statue of mythical sex worker 'Joy' celebrates industry
items.summary: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.

items.title: The Solomons Six were recruited to a 'catch and kill' squad in WWII — their stories were lost to history until now
items.summary: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

items.title: The Australians keeping a century of rainfall records as stations disappear
items.summary: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

items.title: NDIS unregistered provider crackdown coming, as Butler searches for more than $6b in savings
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

items.title: From celebration to calls of weakness, how the Trump-Pope Leo relationship soured
items.summary: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the past year.

items.title: As the world races to shore up oil supplies, Japan promotes a stockpiling model to Asia
items.summary: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

items.title: Why Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration is raising eyebrows
items.summary: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

items.title: How the perfect mother myth was unravelled by a family mystery in Italy
items.summary: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

items.title: Today in history, April 19: Good News Week premiered 30 years ago
items.summary: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.
